original_text: The United States, China, and the European Union sign a trade agreement eliminating all tariffs on clean energy technologies.
identified_property: comprehensiveness and depth of the agreement

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: The United States, China, and the European Union sign a non-binding memorandum of intent to explore future discussions on reducing select tariffs on a narrow category of clean energy technologies.

variations.label: low
variations.text: The United States, China, and the European Union sign a trade agreement modestly reducing tariffs on a limited subset of clean energy technologies, with no timeline for further reductions.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: The United States, China, and the European Union sign a trade agreement significantly reducing, but not fully eliminating, tariffs across most categories of clean energy technologies.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: The United States, China, and the European Union sign a trade agreement eliminating all tariffs on clean energy technologies.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: The United States, China, and the European Union sign a trade agreement eliminating all tariffs on clean energy technologies and harmonizing key regulatory standards to streamline cross-border deployment.

variations.label: high
variations.text: The United States, China, and the European Union sign a trade agreement eliminating all tariffs on clean energy technologies, harmonizing regulatory standards, establishing jointly funded R&D initiatives, and creating intellectual property sharing frameworks to accelerate innovation.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: The United States, China, and the European Union sign a comprehensive trade agreement eliminating all tariffs on clean energy technologies, fully harmonizing regulatory and certification standards, launching jointly funded R&D programs, creating open intellectual property sharing frameworks, instituting binding technology transfer mechanisms for developing nations, and establishing an enforceable dispute resolution body with clear compliance timelines.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier deepens the agreement's scope and binding power: Tier 1 is merely a non-binding intent to talk; Tier 2 introduces limited, real tariff cuts; Tier 3 broadens and deepens reductions but falls short of elimination; Tier 4 (the original) fully eliminates tariffs; Tier 5 adds regulatory harmonization; Tier 6 adds joint R&D and IP cooperation; Tier 7 adds technology transfer to developing nations, full standards alignment, and binding enforcement. The core scenario—a US-China-EU clean energy trade agreement—remains constant throughout.

preference_direction: Greater comprehensiveness and depth makes the agreement more effective at accelerating global clean energy adoption, reducing friction, fostering innovation, and creating durable enforcement mechanisms, making each higher tier strictly more preferable.
